title: Translate a delivered patient-facing summary (domain reviewer)
context: A delivered and signed-off plain-language patient-facing summary (plainlang-019) reaches only English-speaking audiences. Translating it widens reach to patient advocates and patients in other language communities. As a patient-facing document, the translation carries the same riskTier:high as the source and requires sign-off from both an oncology domain reviewer (to confirm clinical accuracy is preserved) and a qualified language reviewer before publication. Zero ungated patient-facing output ships in any language.
objective: Translate one delivered and signed-off plain-language patient-facing summary into a target language, with sign-off from both a credentialed oncology domain reviewer (for accuracy) and a qualified language reviewer (for fluency and cultural appropriateness) recorded before publication.
acceptanceCriteria: Translation is derived only from a fully signed-off plain-language summary (plainlang-019 or equivalent); the source document's oncologist + patient-advocate sign-off is recorded as a prerequisite.
The translated document preserves the 'not medical advice' disclaimer and research-use-only framing in the target language; no diagnostic, prognostic, or treatment guidance is present or implied.

A credentialed oncologist (or equivalent oncology domain expert with language proficiency) has reviewed the translation for clinical accuracy and signed off before publication; their name, affiliation, and sign-off date are recorded in a committed review artifact.

A qualified language reviewer (native or fluent speaker with domain familiarity) has reviewed the translation for fluency and cultural appropriateness and signed off before publication; their name and sign-off date are recorded in the same committed review artifact.
The translation is published only after both sign-offs are recorded; the review artifact is committed alongside the translated document.
The target language is specified and documented at the start of the task.
resources: PLAN.md — Patient-facing layer; Quality, review & risk gates
output: A translated patient-facing summary in the target language, committed with a review artifact recording oncology domain reviewer and language reviewer sign-offs, published only after both sign-offs are recorded.
